services.name: Google Cloud IAM
services.description: Unified identity and access management for GCP resources. Uses a policy model where IAM policies bind members (users, groups, service accounts, domains) to roles at various resource hierarchy levels (organization, folder, project, resource). Supports predefined roles, custom roles, and IAM Conditions for attribute-based access control.

services.name: Security Command Center
services.description: GCP's built-in security and risk management platform. Standard tier provides asset inventory, security misconfiguration scanning, and web application vulnerability scanning. Premium tier adds Event Threat Detection (real-time log analysis), Container Threat Detection, Virtual Machine Threat Detection, and compliance monitoring against CIS, PCI DSS, NIST 800-53, and ISO 27001.

services.name: Google Chronicle Security Operations
services.description: Cloud-native SIEM and SOAR platform backed by Google infrastructure and threat intelligence. Ingests and normalizes security telemetry at petabyte scale with 12-month hot retention. Uses YARA-L detection rules, entity graph analysis, and Mandiant threat intelligence for threat detection and investigation.

services.name: Google Cloud Armor
services.description: DDoS protection and WAF service for applications behind Google Cloud Load Balancers. Provides always-on L3/L4 DDoS protection, preconfigured WAF rules (OWASP Top 10, SQLi, XSS), custom security policies with CEL expressions, adaptive protection using ML, rate limiting, and bot management.

services.name: Cloud Key Management Service (Cloud KMS)
services.description: Managed service for creating, importing, and managing cryptographic keys. Supports symmetric and asymmetric keys for encryption and signing. Integrates with GCP services for customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK). Offers software, hardware (HSM), and external key management options.

services.name: VPC Service Controls
services.description: Creates security perimeters around GCP resources to mitigate data exfiltration risks. Service perimeters restrict API access to GCP services (BigQuery, Cloud Storage, etc.) based on client IP, identity, device attributes, and VPC network. Prevents data movement between projects outside the perimeter.

services.name: Binary Authorization
services.description: Deploy-time security control for GKE and Cloud Run that ensures only trusted container images are deployed. Enforces attestation policies requiring images to be signed by trusted authorities before deployment. Integrates with Artifact Registry vulnerability scanning to block images with critical CVEs.

services.name: Cloud Audit Logs
services.description: Records administrative activities and access to data within GCP resources. Admin Activity logs are always-on and free. Data Access logs record data reads/writes and can be enabled per-service. System Event logs capture GCP system actions. Policy Denied logs capture access denials.

services.name: Secret Manager
services.description: Centralized service for managing secrets such as API keys, passwords, certificates, and other sensitive data. Secrets are stored encrypted and versioned. Supports automatic replication across regions, IAM-based access control, and audit logging of all access. Integrates with GKE, Cloud Run, and Cloud Functions.

services.name: Certificate Authority Service (CA Service)
services.description: Managed private Certificate Authority (CA) service for issuing and managing private certificates. Supports custom certificate profiles, certificate lifecycle management, and integration with GKE for mesh certificates. Enables organizations to run their own PKI without managing CA infrastructure.

services.name: BeyondCorp Enterprise
services.description: Google's zero-trust access platform that enables secure access to applications without a VPN. Evaluates user identity, device security posture, and context (location, time) for every access request. Built on Google's internal BeyondCorp architecture that has protected Google's workforce since 2011.
keyFeatures: Access to web apps, VMs, and APIs

services.name: reCAPTCHA Enterprise
services.description: Bot detection and fraud prevention service that protects web applications from automated attacks, credential stuffing, account takeover, and scraping. Uses adaptive risk analysis based on user behavior signals. Provides risk scores rather than binary challenge-or-pass decisions.

services.name: Web Security Scanner
services.description: Automated web application vulnerability scanner that identifies security issues in App Engine, GKE, and Compute Engine web applications. Scans for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities including XSS, SQL injection, mixed content, and outdated libraries. Findings are reported in Security Command Center.

services.name: Sensitive Data Protection (Cloud DLP)
services.description: Service for discovering, classifying, and protecting sensitive data across GCP and beyond. Scans Cloud Storage, BigQuery, Datastore, and external data sources for 150+ built-in infoTypes (PII, credentials, financial data). Supports de-identification techniques: masking, tokenization, bucketing, and format-preserving encryption.

services.name: Organization Policy Service
services.description: Centralized constraint enforcement across the GCP resource hierarchy. Organization policies set guardrails that apply to all resources under an organization node. Constraints include restricting resource locations, disabling service account key creation, enforcing uniform bucket-level access, and requiring OS Login.

services.name: Cloud IDS (Intrusion Detection System)
services.description: Managed network-based intrusion detection system powered by Palo Alto Networks threat intelligence. Provides visibility into network-based threats including malware, spyware, command-and-control attacks, and other network-based threats. Traffic is mirrored for inspection without inline performance impact.

services.name: Assured Workloads
services.description: Enables customers to apply security and compliance controls to GCP environments for regulated workloads. Creates compliant folders with enforced controls for data residency, personnel access restrictions, and encryption requirements. Supports FedRAMP, ITAR, CJIS, IL4/IL5, and regional compliance packages.

services.name: Security Posture Service
services.description: Defines, deploys, and monitors a security posture across GCP organization. A posture is a set of policy constraints and Security Health Analytics detectors that define an organization's security standards. Detects drift from the defined posture and integrates with Terraform for infrastructure-as-code deployments.
